Ledgely — Small Business Systems

Clear answers, simple tools, and templates for small business owners. Step-by-step guides for EIN, ITIN, business setup, expense tracking, invoicing, and financial clarity.

Free Guides

What is an EIN. Who needs an EIN. EIN Application Preparation. What is an ITIN. ITIN Application Checklist. How to register a small business. How to track business expenses. What is a profit and loss statement. Cash flow basics for small businesses.

Free Tools

Monthly Profit Estimator. Business Expense Tracker. Cash Flow Planner. Invoice Generator.

Pro Systems

Business Starter System. Financial Control System. Client and Invoice Management System.

Small business systems & efficiency

Clear answers, simple tools, and templates for small business owners.

Ledgely is a focused library of plain-English documentation, free in-browser tools, and ready-to-use templates for tax IDs, business setup, and basic financial systems.

Start here →Browse the docsOpen the tools

Your progression

  1. Beginner→
  2. Setup→
  3. Tools→
  4. Templates→
  5. Pro Systems

A note from the founder

I built Ledgely because every time I looked up something simple — like how to get an EIN or what documents to keep on file — I either got a law firm blog trying to sell me services, or a Reddit thread from 2019 with conflicting answers.

Ledgely is the resource I wished existed. Plain answers, correct order of operations, no agenda.

I also believe the businesses that win long-term are the ones that give first — genuinely, without expecting anything back. That's why everything here is free to read, no forms, no paywalls. If it helps you, it was worth publishing.

Your personal story is your most defensible competitive advantage — nobody can copy who you are or how you think. Build a brand, not just a business.

— The Ledgely Team

23 free guides·4 free tools·9 ready-to-use templates·No account needed

Why this matters

Why Ledgely Exists

Most small businesses fail because of two things: poor financial clarity and a messy setup structure.

Ledgely simplifies setup, tracking, and documentation into one connected system — so the basics are no longer a guessing game.

Everything is designed to move you from confusion to control.

Step 1Top priority

Recommended Path (Follow in Order)

Most users start here to go from idea → registered business → first invoice. Each milestone unlocks the next.

  1. 1
    Step 1: EIN DecisionStart here

    Find out if your business actually needs an EIN.

    Begin step 1 →Then unlocks: Step 2 · EIN Preparation
  2. 2
    Step 2: EIN PreparationNext milestone

    Gather every detail you need before you apply.

    Open the checklist →Then unlocks: Step 3 · Business Setup
  3. 3
    Step 3: Business SetupRegister your business

    Form your entity, organize documents, stay compliant.

    Set up the business →Then unlocks: Step 4 · Financial Tracking
  4. 4
    Step 4: Financial TrackingUnderstand your money

    Track expenses and see your real monthly profit.

    Open the tracker →Then unlocks: Step 5 · Invoice System
  5. 5
    Step 5: Invoice SystemStart getting paid

    Send a clean, professional invoice and get paid faster.

    Open the invoice tool →Then unlocks: Step 6 · Templates
  6. 6
    Step 6: TemplatesFinal setup tools

    Grab ready-to-use templates and checklists to lock it in.

    Browse templates →You've completed the recommended path.

Utility layer · In-browser

Tools

Monthly Profit Estimator

Know your real monthly profit after expenses in seconds.

Business Expense Tracker

See exactly where your money goes and what to cut immediately.

Cash Flow Planner

Predict future cash shortages before they happen.

Get Paid Invoice System

Send professional invoices that make clients take payment seriously.

Support layer

Templates

Copy-paste checklists, CSV starters, and document templates to support every step of the recommended path.

What is an EIN?

A clear, plain-English explanation of the Employer Identification Number and what it's used for.

3 min read

EIN

Who needs an EIN?

A quick rundown of which small businesses are required to have an EIN, and which ones simply benefit from one.

EIN

EIN Application Preparation Checklist (Business Tax ID)

Organize the information you'll need before applying for an EIN with the IRS.

6 min read

Why people use Ledgely

What makes Ledgely different from the usual small business advice you'll find online.

Plain English, no jargon

Every guide is written to give you a direct answer in the first two sentences — no filler, no upselling you on software.

Free tools that work in your browser

No account, no download, no signup. Open a tool and use it immediately.

Built around the real order of operations

Most business setup advice skips steps or gets the order wrong. Ledgely follows the actual sequence — structure first, EIN second, bank account third.

How every guide is structured

Pages are written so you can extract an answer in seconds and act on it the same day.

Direct answer

A 1–2 sentence answer at the top of every page.

Simple explanation

Plain-English context in bullet points.

Step-by-step

Numbered actions you can follow today.

Checklist or summary

A quick recap or interactive checklist.

Next step

A direct link to the matching tool, template, or guide.

Ledgely · Practical systems for small business owners.
